title: Buff / Debuff – Basic Guide
summary: How Evony buffs and debuffs actually work: the six buff types, additive stacking, combat-role activation, and the half-reduction debuff rule.
content: Debuffs reduce an enemy's buff by HALF at most (never to 0): -300% vs a 600% buff -> 300%; vs 800% -> 500% (limited by your debuff size). Buff percentages ADD across all sources; final stat = base x (totalBuff% + 100%). Flat (absolute) buffs apply AFTER % and are NOT reduced by debuffs -> flat is better for t1-t11, % better for t12+. Observed maxima: buffs over 2800%, debuffs over -1100%. Six buff types by in-game notation: Basic (always on), Marching/'Attacking', In-City ('Defending City'), Defense ('Defending~', only from Blazon/Military Academy/Castle Decoration), Outcity Defense (only Idunn & Freyja), Reinforcing. Which apply depends on combat role: solo/rally attack & camp-defense = Basic+Marching; own-city defense = Basic+In-City+Defense; building/relic defense or reinforce = Basic+Marching+Defense+Outcity. Stacking: sub-city mayor buffs do NOT reach main-city troops, but same-culture sub-cities' own buffs stack; rally participants' buffs never stack (each applies to its own troops). Debuffs: mayor debuffs DO stack when SOLO (sub-city must be alive/in battle), but in a RALLY only the single strongest debuffer applies (-70% + -140% -> only -140%). Max 9 subordinate cities.

title: Debuff Simulator
summary: Interactive tool listing every debuff source with per-item magnitudes to compute total troop-type debuffs.
content: Enumerates all debuff sources with magnitudes. Sub-city caps in the tool: 12 (equipment), 11 (mayor). Red-equipment debuffs run -33% to -40% per piece (Achaemenidae Sword: Mounted Atk -40%; Ares Axe: Ranged HP -33%); Achaemenidae 6/6 set = All Troops Attack -15%. Specialty debuffs per general, Lv1->Lv5 = -1/-2/-4/-6/-10% (Snipe = enemy All Attack, Sabotage = All Defense, Suppress = All HP). Blazon debuff sets: attack sets 2/6 = -20%, 4/6 = -20%/-20%; defense sets 2/6 = -25%, 4/6 = -25%/-25%. Civilization Treasure single-type Lv1-5: -5/-12.5/-25/-37.5/-50%. Spiritual Beast single stat: Gray Lv1 -2% -> Gold Lv10 -30% -> Red Lv15 -52%. Academy research maxed hits Lv35 A140/D70/H70 (absolute). Sub-city mayors give -10% to -50% type debuffs (Narses: Ground Atk -50%). Duty officers: Ban Chao Mounted Atk -20%, others -15%. Strategy: equip ALL sub-city mayors with red debuff gear; debuffs stack when solo.

title: List of Subordinate City Buff
summary: The two buffs each subordinate-city culture grants per rarity tier (Common->Epic); same-culture cities stack additively.
content: Each culture gives two buffs scaling by rarity Common/Uncommon/Epic/Legendary/Epic-top (values shown Common->top): China Resource Production +4->20%, Training Speed +2->10%. Korea Warehouse +6->30%, Extra Gather Resources +3->15%. Japan Attacking Troop Attack (when marching) +2->10%, Research-Factory Material +1->5%. Europe Construction +3->15%, General EXP from Monster kill +2->10%. America (this sub-city) Gold Production +10->50%, Research Speed +3->15%. Russia In-City Troop Attack (when defending own city) +2->10%, Trap Attack +3->15%. Arabia Hospital Capacity +3->15%, Healing Speed +2->10%. Any culture: Training Speed in that sub-city (needs Historic mayor) +2->20%; Death-into-Survival rate in that sub-city (needs mayor Leadership+Politics >=900) +2->10%. Same-culture cities stack additively. Combat picks: Japan = stacked marching attack (offense); Russia = in-city defense attack + traps (defense). Note mayor BUFFS stay in their sub-city; only mayor DEBUFFS project to fights.

title: Rank Type, Buff, Requirement List
summary: The seven monarch ranks, their prestige/VIP requirements, and the flat buffs each grants.
content: Seven ranks Knight->Regent, each unlocked by EITHER prestige OR a VIP level (VIP13-19). Prestige thresholds: Knight 5,000; Baron 20,000; Viscount 65,000; Earl 163,000; Duke 343,000; Archduke 703,000; Regent 3,000,000. Rank buffs (Knight->Regent): Training Speed / Healing Speed / March Speed / Traps Building Speed each +5/+10/+15/+20/+30/+50/+70%; General Capacity -/+1/+2/+4/+6/+9/+14; Subordinate City Capacity +1/+2/+3/+4/+5/+6/+6; March Size Capacity +5,000/+10,000/+15,000/+20,000/+30,000/+50,000/+100,000. Strategy levers: higher rank raises march size, adds subordinate-city slots (more mayors = more stackable debuffs), and lifts general capacity. Duke (+30% training/healing/march, +5 sub-cities) is the common mid target; Regent needs 3,000,000 prestige.

title: Best Skill Books Guide - What Skills Should You Add?
summary: Which skill books to attach per role for monster hunting, PvP attack/defense, gathering, and support duties.
content: A general fills 6 skill-book slots (main + assistant combined); Mayor and Duty Officer have only 3 (no assistant). 'Develop Strengths' rule: Mounted->Attack, Ranged & Siege->Range, Ground->HP & Defense. Range and Speed can ONLY be raised by skill books, so they are high priority; Range books work for ranged & siege only, Speed books for mounted & ground only. PvP loadout per role: Attack, Defense, HP, March Size, March Speed, plus Range (ranged/siege) or Speed (mounted/ground). Siege HP/Defense books add ONLY 25% -> low priority. Monster hunting (mounted-based, one-round kill): Luck, Mounted-Attack-vs-Monster, Mounted Attack, March Size, then vs-Monster HP/Defense. HP is preferred over Defense (fewer injuries); Defense is nearly meaningless vs bosses (a 5000% Defense buff barely reduces boss damage). Wall/Defense: ranged & siege Range/Attack plus ground Speed/Defense. Mayor: Rebirth, Training Speed, Training Capacity. Gatherer: the 4 gatherer-speed books + March Speed. Troop-type buff books do NOT work on Mayor/Duty Officer.

title: Skillbook List (Lv1-4 Effects)
summary: Complete numeric table of every skill book (Military, Development, Sub-City, Debuff, Duty) with Lv1-4 magnitudes.
content: Military books Lv1/2/3/4: March Size 3/6/9/12%; March Speed 10/15/20/25%; Renascence 15/25/50/75%; troop Speed (per type) 5/10/15/20%; every troop-type Attack, Defence, HP 10/15/20/25%; Ranged Range +25/50/75/100 (flat); Siege Range +50/100/150/200 (flat). Development books: Gatherer (per resource) 15/25/35/45%; Troop Load (per type) 15/25/35/45%; Luck (double drop) 5/10/15/18%; Monster's Respect (EXP) 10/15/20/25%; Monster March Speed 15/25/35/45%; Against-Monster Atk/Def/HP (per type) 15/25/35/45%. Subordinate-City books: Rebirth 1/2/3/5%; Construction 15/25/35/45%; Gold Production 15/25/35/45%; Training Capacity 15/25/35/45%; Training Speed 10/15/20/25%. Debuff books (enemy type Atk/Def/HP): -2/-5/-9/-15%. Duty-officer books add small in-rally / defending-troop buffs (e.g. Forge Officer In-Rally Ground 2/4/10/20%). Key takeaways: Lv4 is worth ~2.5x Lv1; Luck maxes at 18%; troop-type combat books cap 25% at Lv4.

title: Specialty Guide
summary: General specialties (4 on gold, 3 on purple) plus the Flexible 5th Specialty, with runestone/gem costs and F2P feasibility.
content: Gold generals have 4 specialties, purple have 3; unlocked at general Lv25 (start value 0). Each slot needs a specific runestone: 1=Leadership, 2=Military, 3=Glory, 4=Immortal (purple lacks slot 4). Five levels Green/Blue/Purple/Orange/Gold. Per-step cost (runestones / gems): Green 200/4,000; Blue 500/10,000; Purple 1,000/19,980; Orange 3,000/60,000; Gold 8,000/160,000. Maxing all 4 = 50,800 runestones + ~1,015,920 gems. Runestone-chest drop odds: Leadership ~34%, Military ~32%, Glory ~24%, Immortal ~10% (Immortal is the slot-4 bottleneck). Cost tracks: pure pay ~$6,350; Shopping-Spree route ~$1,600 over ~19 months; F2P ~32 months (2-3 years). Advice: pour runestones into ONE general first; the 4th specialty is for heavy payers and only unlocks after the first 3 are all gold. Flexible (5th) Specialty: 10 types, unlocks when the 3 left specialties are gold, levels to 220, 6 qualities (green->red); 'Battle-Tested' is the F2P-friendly one; e.g. Piercing Arrows at MAX = +95% Ranged HP/Def and +140% Ranged Attack.

title: General Skill - How to Add the 3 Skills You Want
summary: A reliable method to place 3 desired Lv4 skills on a general, working around the overwrite mechanics.
content: A general holds 3 chosen skill-book slots. Add success rate: 100% for the 1st skill, 50% for the 2nd, 25% for the 3rd - and a FAILED add overwrites one existing skill. A higher-level book overwrites the same skill at 100%; you CANNOT add a lower-level book of a skill already present (this also blocks a golden general's native skill, e.g. Baibars' double-drop blocks the Luck book). Reliable method: first fill all 3 slots with Lv1 versions of your final skills, THEN overwrite each with the Lv4 book -> guarantees 3 desired Lv4 skills. Decide the final 3 before starting; keep >=3 Lv1 books of each (filling 3 slots took ~9 books). Extra books cost 3,000 gems each at the Academy skill-book shop. Exceptions: some generals accept only Lv4 books (Khalid = Siege Machine Attack, Barbarossa = March Size), so the Lv1-first trick fails for them.

title: Blazon
summary: Blazons equip at the four training facilities to add large troop-type Attack/Defense/HP buffs; 48 blazons across 8 sets and 6 elements, max Lv20.
content: 48 blazons = 8 sets x 6 elemental types (Light/Shadow/Wind/Fire/Ocean/Earth). Equip once a training facility (stable / archer camp / barracks / workshop) reaches Lv8; up to 6 slots per facility (all 6 at facility Lv29). Sets by troop type: Ground=Justice/Sacrifice, Ranged=Valor/Compassion, Mounted=Honor/Soul, Siege=Honesty/Humility. Each troop type can equip 2 blazons each of Attack, Defense, and HP. Two buff layers: each blazon's own (random) buff plus set buffs that activate at 2/6, 4/6, 6/6 pieces of one set. Blazons start with 2 buffs at Lv1, gain a 3rd at Lv10 and a 4th at Lv15; max level 20. The page states you can reach 'nearly 100%' attack/defense/HP buffs at Lv15. Upgrade by feeding other blazons for EXP: Lv1->10 needs 119 Lv1 pieces, Lv1->15 needs 349, Lv1->20 needs 874 (Lv2+ blazons return 80% EXP as material). Blazons are among the biggest F2P combat levers; farm via monster kills and events. Prioritize your main troop type.

title: How to Get Blazon
summary: Every source for blazon pieces, from free monster/event routes to paid packages.
content: F2P/event sources: Trial of Sphinx (kill Sphinx, ~1 piece/kill, up to 12/day; ~24 per 2-day event, up to 36 via a rally-hold trick; daily alliance-score Rank 1 = 2 pieces, Rank 2 = 1). Hecate's Moon: Sphinx kills plus 1 piece per 150 Radiant Night Torches (max 10). Revelation of Horus (9 days): top-50 daily ranking gives 1st=4, 2-10th=3, 11-50th=2, up to 36/event. Alliance Duel Shop: 1 blazon per 300 Duel Coins. Daily Tavern activity: 1/day at 110 score. Auction: 55,000 gems minimum for 10 pieces (event). Voyage to Civilizations: free daily gacha spin plus exchange. Paid: Event Package $9.99=5 / $19.99=10 / $49.99=24 / $99.99=48; Ancient Dynasty Treasury $35=84; King's Party Lv4=5 up to Lv9=40; Sulis Wishing Pool up to 200 pieces. F2P priority: Sphinx events + Horus ranking + Alliance Duel shop.

title: Monarch Gear Guide
summary: Overview of monarch gear and the composition-count/store-cost tables for building pieces Lv1->Lv15.
content: Monarch gear grants buffs for both battle and development; higher level = stronger buff. Leveling = combine THREE same-level pieces into one of the next level. Lv1 pieces required per level: Lv2=3, Lv3=9, Lv4=27, Lv5=81, Lv6=243, ... Lv10=19,683, Lv15=4,782,969. Store price: 200 gems per Lv1 piece (300 for Ares gear). Composing one Lv15 piece purely by buying at the store = 956,593,800 gems (non-Ares) or 1,434,890,700 (Ares) - absurd, so never build from the store. Intermediate store costs: Lv5 = 16,200 / 24,300 gems; Lv10 = 3,936,600 / 5,904,900. This hub page does not list individual piece buff values (see the usage/level-up guides). Practical path is patrols + black market + Wheel of Fortune (see level-up guide).

title: Fastest & Cheapest Way to Level Up Monarch Gear
summary: Cheap monarch-gear leveling via patrols, black market, and the daily Wheel of Fortune instead of store purchases.
content: Compose three same-level pieces into the next level (one Lv5 = 81 Lv1 pieces). Cheap sourcing, in priority order: (1) patrol incessantly - the single most important source; (2) buy monarch gear whenever it shows on the Black Market; (3) spin the Wheel of Fortune daily. Patrol method: refresh with gold until a gear appears, then buy it for 30 gems; do ~30-100 refreshes/day. About 100 refreshes yields 30-40 pieces for ~1,000 gems (vs 24,000+ gems at the store). Black Market Lv3 gear appears at ~200-600 gems/item - but do NOT buy Intellect Crown or Strength Crown there (they cost the same 1,800 gems as the store). Wheel of Fortune gives random Lv2/Lv3 gear (10 spins = 900 chips; also a tavern activity quota). Timeline for free/low spenders: Lv5 gear in 1-2 months, Lv6 in 3-4 months.

title: How to Use Monarch Gear
summary: Correct timing: swap monarch gear per activity and equip only at the trigger moment, because buffs lock in when the action starts or troops arrive.
content: Two rules: (A) change gear per activity; (B) equip only for the moment you need it. Crowns map to action speed: Strength=construction, Intellect=research, Agile=crafting equipment, Martial=training troops, Medicine=healing/siege repair, Durable=building traps. Horns: War Horn=march size/capacity, Courage Horn=march speed, Justice Horn=march speed to monsters, Loyalty Horn=resources carried. Crystals: gather-speed crystals = Light/Wood/Thunder/Golden (typed per resource tile); resource-recovery = Earth/Water/Wind/Fire; production-speedup crystals are the exception you leave equipped. Combat gear (staff/grail/decoration) swaps per fight: hunt monsters with cavalry-type Thunder set; for PvP match your main unit (ranged = Wind). CRITICAL timing: a construction/research/etc. buff applies ONLY if equipped exactly when the action starts - equipping after start does nothing, and you may remove it immediately after starting. Gathering/carry buffs (crystals, Loyalty Horn) lock in only when troops ARRIVE at the resource tile, then can be removed. Save presets via the armor icon (upper-left of the equipment screen).

title: Civilization Equipment - Recommended Combinations
summary: Ranks Civilization (Star Atlas) gear sets by role and compares mixed vs unified builds; the strongest combat gear tier.
content: Civilization (Star Atlas) gear is the strongest tier; all figures are 5-star, marching, with dragons. By role: RANGED - mix recommended, best = Thebes+Plantagenet+Sassane (463% total buff / 320% all-type debuff). MOUNTED - mixed vs unified even: unified Bourbon = better buffs, Han Dynasty = better debuffs (Han 4/6 and 6/6 each give +100% debuff). GROUND - mix, debuff-oriented preferred: Aztec+Furinkazan+Antonine = 609% buff / 410% all-type debuff. SIEGE - unify Heian (better buffs) or Abbas (better debuffs). DEFENSE - unify Koryo (total buff 1170% / debuff 235%). Set-piece bonuses apply at 2/6, 4/6, 6/6 (e.g. 6/6 Aztec/Bourbon/Heian +20/20/20; Furinkazan 6/6 +100% debuff; Koryo 6/6 +200% total). 'Own Full Set' passives apply even when mixing (which is why mixing is viable). Page does not address F2P vs paid [VERIFY]; Civilization gear is crafted at the Wonder (not the Forge), Keep Lv33.

title: Imperial / Parthian Equipment Guide
summary: Upgrading Ares->Imperial and Achaemenidae->Parthian: modest ~3-8% stat gain, forge cost, and whether to prioritize attacker or mayor gear.
content: Strength tier: Ares & Achaemenidae < Imperial & Parthian < Civilization. Imperial/Parthian are only ~3-8% better on buff values than Ares/Achaemenidae (buffs ~+3%, debuffs ~+7% per item); refinement caps are unchanged (attack still 25%), so existing refines carry over and need not be redone. Craft at the Forge: Ares->Imperial (Forge Lv30), Achaemenidae->Parthian (needs Forge Lv33, since Achaemenidae itself is Lv33); un-equip first. Cost per piece: Forge Master Certificate (25 for Imperial, 50 for Parthian) + Lv7 red Materials (4 types x10) + 100 Badges + 10M Gold. Set bonuses - Imperial: Attacking Troop Attack 15%->18%, Ground & Mounted Atk & Def vs Monster 25%->30%, March Size 10%->12%. Parthian: All Troops Attack 10%->12%, Monster Atk & Def 25%->30%, Enemy Troop Atk -15%->-18%. Priority: with little Civ gear, upgrade attackers/defenders; with plenty of Civ gear, prioritize a full Parthian set on the MAYOR (debuffs) - it adds 'hundreds of percent' total debuff.

title: Red Equipment Combination List (Which Should You Make?)
summary: Recommended Dragon/Ares/Achaemenidae red-equipment mixes per general role, including the debuff-only mayor build.
content: Policy: Attack general = Buff, Defense general = Buff, Mayor = Debuff; among base stats prioritize Attack. Named per-role combos exist for Ranged, Ranged+Siege, Ground, Mounted, Monster-Hunt, Siege, Wall Defense, and Mayor (excludes Imperial/Parthian/Civ). Key set rules: full Ares/Achaemenidae set = All Troops Attack +25% when attacking (+ Ground & Mounted Atk & Def +25% vs monsters); full Achaemenidae set = All Troops Attack -15% (LOST if you mix any Ares in). Monster-hunt build swaps in the Courageous Dragon Ring (only ring with double-item-drop) but its set buff drops to +15% All Attack. MAYOR is debuff-only on an Achaemenidae base: Axe (only Ranged-HP debuff), Armor (only Mounted-Def debuff), Fearless Ring (only Mounted-HP debuff). Mayor craft order: Courageous Achaemenidae Ring -> Helmet -> Axe/Spear -> rest. Full mayor debuff targets reach Ranged Atk -600%+, Siege Atk -600%+, Ground HP -600%+, Ground Def -450%+, Mounted HP/Def -300%+ (all capped at HALF the enemy's buffs).

title: Red Equipment Comparison (Dragon, Ares, Achaemenidae)
summary: Per-slot comparison of the three red-equipment lines, their forge tiers, debuff direction, and refine efficiency.
content: Craft tiers by Forge level: Lv27 = Dragon (scroll), Lv30 = Ares (upgrade from Dragon, un-equip first), Lv33 = Achaemenidae (scroll); Civilization = Keep Lv33. Strength: Dragon is weakest; Ares and Achaemenidae are equal strength but opposite direction - and ONLY Ares & Achaemenidae carry debuffs (Ares = attacking, Achaemenidae = defending + mayor debuffs). REFINE PRIORITY: max extra refine buff = 80% on Dragon vs 100% on Ares/Achaemenidae, so do NOT refine Dragon gear - upgrade it to Ares first (Dragon 4x +20% = +80% vs Ares 4x +25% = +100%). 5-star weapons give +30% troop attack on Ares/Ach (vs +25% Dragon); Achaemenidae weapons/protectors also add -20% enemy debuffs. Only the Courageous Dragon Ring has Double Item Drop (+20%). Ares cost per piece = 800 Badges + 30M Gold + a building requirement (e.g. Ring needs 6 Epic Historic generals at ~3.5M power). Dragon needs 15 of each Lv7 material; Ares/Achaemenidae need 20-40.

title: Forge
summary: The Forge crafts and upgrades general equipment; equipment tiers unlock by Forge level.
content: The Forge crafts general equipment from Materials + Scrolls (built at Keep Lv7; Forge Lv26+ also requires raising the Watchtower). Equipment tiers unlock by Forge level: Lv5 Purple, Lv9 Orange, Lv13 Champion's, Lv17 General's, Lv21 King's, Lv27 Red Dragon, Lv30 Ares/Imperial, Lv33 Achaemenidae/Parthian; Duty Officer gear at Lv27/28/33. Notes stated on the page: Champion's (Lv13) 4-piece set adds a resource bonus after gathering; King's (Lv21) Fearless Ring is the most cost-effective double-item-drop piece; Achaemenidae (Lv33, set of 6) is essential mayor debuff gear at K33+. Equipment Upgrade (unlocked Forge Lv30) turns Dragon->Ares and Ares/Achaemenidae->Imperial/Parthian (un-equip first). Speed crafting via Academy 'Blacksmith' research (needs Academy Lv14), equipping the Agile Crown before crafting, and assigning a Forge Duty Officer (Lv28). Materials come mainly from Vikings (best in alliance rallies), Relics, and events. Civilization equipment is crafted at the Wonder, not the Forge.

title: How to Get Forge Master Certificate
summary: The certificate upgrades Ares->Imperial and Achaemenidae->Parthian; obtained mainly from the Gather Troops event and Vikings.
content: The Forge Master Certificate upgrades Ares & Achaemenidae equipment into Imperial & Parthian at the Forge. Two main sources. Gather Troops event: up to 40 pieces via Luxury Treasure Chests keyed to completed quests - 5 quests=10, 10=10, 30=10, 50=10 (so 50 quests = the 40-piece max); complete non-soldier-creation quests to save resources. Vikings: rewarded by the highest own-Viking level cleared after the event - Hard gives up to 10 (Lv30-34=2, 45-49=6, Lv50=10), Hell up to 20 (Lv20-29=3, 40-44=12, Lv50=20); Easy/Normal give none. Guidance: if you can't kill Hell Lv40+, complete Hard Lv50 instead. F2P priority: complete 50 Gather Troops quests + push the highest Viking difficulty you can clear.

title: How to Get Refining Stone
summary: Refining Stones refine equipment; no large single source except paying, so gather steadily from many events and bosses.
content: Refining Stones refine equipment. Amounts per refinement: Ares 200, Dragon-Celtic Demon 50, Dragon-Norway Ridge 100 - so don't waste them. Top steady sources: Crazy Eggs event (>=120/day, up to 360+ if all 4 eggs opened); Gather Troops after-event chests (1 chest=15, 10=150, 20=300); King's Path Day-4 Wheel of Fortune. Boss 'Pattern A' guaranteed chests: Lava Turtle/Golem/Witch/Warlord Lv1-5 = x3/6/9/10/12; Hydra Lv1-4 = x3/5/8/12; Ymir Lv1-5 = x4/8/12/13/15 (plus 1 stone per battle). Weekly Killing Ranking top-100: 50 (51-100th) up to 100 (1st). Viking Hard 50 kills=120. Consuming Return (gem consumption): 50,000 gems=100 up to 2,000,000 gems=3,450 total. Black Market 10=1,500 gems / 50=7,500. Use double-drop generals (Baibars + King's Ring) to farm the boss drops.

title: Seal
summary: Seals are a Spiritual-Beast buff system like Academy research: always-on buffs that need no beast assigned to a general.
content: Seals work like Academy research - they unlock/strengthen an always-in-effect buff and do NOT need the beast assigned to a general (so they cost no dragon/beast slot). Only 2nd- and 3rd-generation beasts raise seals; Seals unlock when a beast reaches Lv10. Each beast has 5 Seals, each raisable to Lv25 (cap raised from 20 to 25 on 11/7/2025), plus 4 Innate Skills that unlock as total Seal level + Pasture level rise (e.g. Innate I needs Seal-total 10 / Pasture 30). At all Seals Lv25 a beast reaches lines like +62.5% to +80% Attack/Def/HP, +26% troop bundles, plus march/gathering/production buffs and rally/defense debuffs. Cost rises 'like a quadratic curve' while the buff gained flattens, so level ALL beasts evenly (every beast to 10, then 15, then 20) rather than maxing one. Combat picks: PvP ranged = Otso/Hati/Thunderbird/Nine-Tailed Fox; PvP defense = Hati/Chiron/Pegasus; monster = Tarasque/Chrysomallos/Nandi/Pegasus/Chiron. Powered by Gems + Blood Crystal (resource tiles Lv13+ max 20/day; bosses max 40/day).
